“我们应该从政府未能升级系统以及从锁定生活中汲取经验教训。两者都显示出免费的公共宽带不仅仅是社会主义，而且-通俗地说是一位对技术创新了如指掌的部长托尼·本恩（Tony Benn），这也是常识。
  英国广播公司（BBC）称其为“宽带共产主义”，鲍里斯·约翰逊（Boris Johnson）称其为“ crackpot”计划，而前工党影子大臣（Change Shadow UK）候选人，前劳工影子大臣克里斯·莱斯利（Chris Leslie）开玩笑说“为什么不扔免费的Sky TV？免费iPhone？’
 但是自工党于2019年12月承诺提供免费的全光纤宽带以来，情况已经发生了变化。由于近代史上最严重的流行病迫使我们进入家中，甚至在网上进一步普及，誓言并不是关于我们的数字技术的有远见的声明基础设施应该看起来像是，而且是使所有人在公共卫生危机中生活，学习和工作的基本先决条件。
 距离大选已经过去了一年，而且我们在封锁之下越来越依赖宽带接入，这加剧了人们对“数字鸿沟”的担忧。在嘲笑工党2019年的宣言承诺之后，记者和政客们-正确地说，如果是迟来的话-震惊地引用了ONS的数字，即51％收入在6000到1万英镑的家庭无法使用网络。
 最近，盖文·威廉姆森（Gavin Williamson）拒绝接受英国电信（BT）提供的免费券，这些券用于在公共卫生危机期间没有互联网连接的儿童，因此遭到批评。教育部在回应中声称，提供的10Mbps连接速度太慢，无法播放视频等教学资料。
 显然，这是一个明智的论点-任何连接总比没有连接好-但同样令人不安的是，我们依靠私营公司从他们内心的“善意”中提供不合标准的宽带速度。正如克莱门特·艾德礼（Clement Attlee）所说，“慈善是一件冷酷，灰色，无情的事情”。
 随着我们越来越多的生活在线上，从教育到公共服务的一切都通过宽带连接来提供，我们不必依靠私人互联网提供商的慷慨慷慨来保证什么是基本的访问权。 
每年流量增加40％，这也意味着我们需要升级系统-2018年国家基础设施评估估计，我们将在2030年至2040年之间达到现有的部分铜，部分光纤网络的容量。
 政府依靠私营部门用全光纤千兆（1000Mbps）电缆取代我们80％的超高速30Mbps网络，而其余20％（包括难以到达或偏远地区）将由数字文化部提供补贴。媒体和体育（DCMS）。部长们希望在2025年前提供完整的光纤覆盖范围。
 他们的方法行不通。去年年底，在未能达到关键里程碑后，该目标被下调至85％的覆盖率。此后，DCMS一直忽略发布任何路线图来满足新的截止日期，并且没有计划或什至目标日期来规划该部门在2025年之后如何以及何时实现100％的覆盖率。目前，只有14％的网络已满，光纤–欧洲覆盖率最低的国家之一。最有可能被联系的人们（偏远的农村社区中的人们）很可能会被完全忽略。
  令人难以接受的是，美国商务部再次在全国范围内设定了覆盖率目标（现已到2025年从100％修订为85％），但尚未发布切合实际的策略，并且对杠杆的控制有限实现它所必需的。
 PAC批评部长们对部署“有限的控制权”，以及未能按时完成任务，这是采用政府以市场为主导的数字基础设施建设方法的主要原因之一。在DCMS委托撰写的2018年报告中，介绍了不同方式升级系统的成本和相对优点，作者认为，市场驱动的方法是实现2025年目标的唯一方法。该说法已证明是错误的。
 因此，工党在2019年做出的承诺不仅是免费提供宽带接入，而且是将BT Openreach国有化以发展全光纤容量的原因是正确的。私营部门的部署未能提供我们将我们带入下一个十年所需的网络。如果不能，则公共部门必须这样做。
 与其将重要基础设施的重要决策交给私人公司，还不如需要一个公有的宽带网络，该宽带网络是根据社会需要而不是私人利益进行规划和升级的。而不是依靠公司的慈善机构，访问该网络应该是一项权利，而不仅限于那些有能力负担的人。 
我们应该从政府未能升级系统以及在封锁中的生活经验中吸取教训。 两者都表明，免费的公共宽带不仅是社会主义的，而且-通俗地说，是一位对技术创新了如指掌的部长托尼·本恩（Tony Benn），这也是常识。  萨姆·布罗（Sam Browse）是反对紧缩劳动大会，复活节（Arise Festival）的积极分子，也是《劳工展望》的定期撰稿人。
note_en:
“We should learn from the government’s failure to upgrade the system, and from our experience of life under lockdown. Both have shown free, public broadband isn’t just socialism, but – to paraphrase a Minister who knew all about technical innovation, Tony Benn – it’s also common sense.”
  The BBC called it ‘broadband communism’, Boris Johnson called it a ‘crackpot’ plan, and Chris Leslie – a former Labour Shadow Chancellor turned Change UK candidate – joked ‘why not throw in free Sky TV? Free iPhones?’
 But things have changed since Labour made its promise to deliver free, full-fibre broadband in December 2019.  As the most serious pandemic in recent history forces us into our homes and even further online, the pledge is less a visionary statement about what our digital infrastructure should look like, and more a basic precondition for enabling everyone to live, learn and work during the public health crisis.
 Fast forward a year from the general election, and our increased reliance on broadband access under lockdown has amplified concern for the “digital divide”. After mocking Labour’s 2019 manifesto commitment, journalists and politicians – rightly, if belatedly –  quote with shock  the ONS figure that 51% of households with an income of £6-10k are without access to the web.
 Recently, Gavin Williamson faced a chorus of criticism for rejecting an offer from BT of free vouchers for children who are without an internet connection during the public health crisis. In their response, the Department for Education claimed that the 10Mbps connection speeds on offer were too slow to stream teaching materials, such as videos.
 It’s obviously a risible argument – any connection is better than no connection – but it’s also a travesty that we’re relying on private corporations to supply sub-standard broadband speeds out of the “kindness” of their hearts. As Clement Attlee said, ‘charity is a cold, grey, loveless thing’.
 As more of our lives move online and everything from education to public service provision is supplied through the medium of a broadband connection, we shouldn’t have to rely on the meagre generosity of private internet providers to guarantee what should be a basic right of access.
 A 40% increase in traffic each year also means we need to upgrade the system – the 2018  National infrastructure Assessment estimates that we’ll reach capacity for the existing part-copper, part-fibre network between 2030 and 2040.
 The Government is relying on the private sector to replace 80% of our superfast 30Mbps network with full-fibre gigabyte (1000Mbps) cables, while the other 20% – comprising hard to reach or remote areas – will be subsidised by the Department for Digital Culture Media and Sport (DCMS). Ministers  had aspired to provide full fibre coverage by 2025.
 Their approach isn’t working. Late last year, after a failure to reach key milestones, the target was revised downward to 85% coverage. DCMS has since neglected to publish any roadmap for meeting the new deadline, and there are no plans – or even target dates – for how and when the department will achieve 100% coverage after 2025. Currently, only 14% of the network is full-fibre – one of the lowest rates of coverage in Europe. There’s a good chance that the people who most need to be connected – people in isolated rural communities – will be completely overlooked.
  It is unacceptable that, once again, the Department has set a nationwide target for coverage (now revised from 100% to 85% by 2025), yet it has not published a realistic strategy for rolling it out, and has limited control over the levers necessary to achieve it.
 PAC’s criticism of Ministers’ ‘limited control’ over the rollout and the failure to meet the deadline demolish one of the key reasons for adopting the Government’s market-led approach to developing digital infrastructure.  In a 2018 report commissioned by DCMS on the costs and relative merits of different ways of upgrading the system, the authors argued that a market-driven approach was the only way to meet the 2025 target. That claim has proven demonstrably false.
 And that’s why Labour’s 2019 pledge not only to provide free access to broadband, but to nationalise BT Openreach to develop full-fibre capacity was right. The private sector rollout is failing to deliver the network we need to take us into the next decade. If it can’t, the public sector must.
 Rather than putting important decisions about key infrastructure in the hands of private companies, we need a publicly owned broadband network which is planned and upgraded on the basis of social need, not private profits; and rather than relying on the charity of corporations, access to that network should be a right, not reserved only for those that can afford it.
 We should learn from the government’s failure to upgrade the system, and from our experience of life under lockdown. Both have shown that free, public broadband isn’t just socialism, but – to paraphrase a Minister who knew all about technical innovation, Tony Benn – it’s also common sense.
 Sam Browse is an activist with the Labour Assembly Against Austerity, Arise Festival, and a regular contributor to Labour Outlook.
title: 大流行表明，免费的公共宽带不仅仅是社会主义； 这是常识
title_en: The pandemic has shown free, public broadband isn’t just socialism; it’s common sense

这项工作仍处于初期阶段，但是实验室种植的植物材料的潜在应用意义重大，包括在农业和合成材料中的可能性。尽管与畜牧业相比，传统农业对生态的破坏要小得多，但它仍会产生重大影响和成本，并且需要大量资源进行维护。更不用说即使很小的环境变化也会对作物产量产生重大影响。
 同时，林业对环境的负面影响更为明显。如果这些研究人员的工作最终可用于创建一种可扩展且高效的方式来生产用于建筑和制造的实验室种植木材的方法，那么在减少全球林业影响方面具有巨大潜力。最终，团队甚至认为您可以哄骗植物性材料的生长成特定的目标形状，因此您也可以在实验室中进行某些制造，例如直接种植木桌。
  研究人员所取得的成就还有很长的路要走。他们仅以很小的规模种植材料，并且将寻求解决方法来种植具有不同最终特性的植物材料，这是一项挑战。他们还需要克服在扩展效率方面的重大障碍，但是他们正在研究可以解决其中一些困难的解决方案。
 实验室种植的肉仍处于起步阶段，实验室种植的植物材料更是新生。但是，即使到达目的地需要很长时间，它也具有巨大的潜力。
note_en:
This work is still in its very early stages, but the potential applications of lab-grown plant material are significant, and include possibilities in both agriculture and in ruction materials. While traditional agricultural is much less ecologically damaging when compared to animal farming, it can still have a significant impact and cost, and it takes a lot of resources to maintain. Not to mention that even small environmental changes can have a significant effect on crop yield.
 Forestry, meanwhile, has much more obvious negative environmental impacts. If the work of these researchers can eventually be used to create a way to produce lab-grown wood for use in construction and fabrication, in a way that’s scalable and efficient, then there’s tremendous potential in terms of reducing the impact of forestry globally. Eventually, the team even theorizes you could coax the growth of plant-based materials into specific target shapes, so you could also do some of the manufacturing in the lab, by growing a wood table directly for instance.
  There’s still a long way to go from what the researchers have achieved. They’ve only grown materials on a very small scale, and will look to figure out ways to grow plant-based materials with different final properties as one challenge. They’ll also need to overcome significant barriers when it comes to scaling efficiencies, but they are working on solutions that could address some of these difficulties.
 Lab-grown meat is still in its infancy, and lab-grown plant material is even more nascent. But it has tremendous potential, even if it takes a long time to get there.
title: 麻省理工学院开发了实验室种植的植物的方法，最终导致了林业和农业的替代
title_en: MIT develops method for lab-grown plants that eventually lead to alternatives to forestry and farming

（CNN）在本月爆发的Covid-19疫情已导致数千万人受到严格封锁之后，中国急于建立一个可容纳4000多人的大规模隔离营。
 隔离营地位于河北省省会石家庄市郊，周围环绕着首都北京。
 中国在很大程度上遏制了该病毒的传播，该国大部分地区已恢复正常。但是，案件的突然增加使官员感到震惊，并引起了人们的关注，农历新年是该县最重要的年度节日，在此期间，预计将有成千上万人前往探望家人。
 疫情集中的石家庄的官员已开始进行大规模检测和严格的封锁措施，将整个村庄转移到集中检疫设施中，以遏制病毒的传播。
  随着当局继续进行广泛的接触者追踪和测试计划，新的隔离营将容纳已确认的Covid-19患者的密切接触者。
  最初计划容纳3,000人，但此后已扩大为4,160人。 4,000多名建筑工人进行了为期6天的作业。和夜晚工作＆＃34;石家庄市副市长孟祥红周二表示，要完成第一阶段工作。
 国有广播公司CCTV称，当局于1月13日开始建设，该营地的第一部分现已完成并可以使用，而第二阶段的建设仍在继续。 
据中央电视台称，每个预制房间的面积预计为18平方米（约194平方英尺），并配有连接浴室和淋浴，书桌，椅子，床，无线网络和电视机。
  这项雄心勃勃的任务使人想起了大流行初期的早期努力，在此期间，当局从头开始建造了几处医疗设施，其中包括在短短10天内就拥有1000张床的医院。
 周二，中国报告了103例新确诊病例和58例无症状感染，分别计算在四个省份。根据省卫生委员会的数据，河北省目前共有818例在当地传播的活跃病例，并有200多种无症状感染。
 上周三，一名患者在河北省死亡，这是该国242天以来首例与Covid-19相关的死亡。
 目前中国大陆确诊的Covid-19病例总数为88,557，官方死亡人数为4,635。
   为了遏制疫情，当局于1月8日将石家庄置于封锁之下，所有1100万居民被禁止离开该市。
 中国国家媒体CGTN上周报道，作为预防措施，自石江庄12个村庄的2万多市民已被转移到其他检疫场所。 
迄今为止，河北已经进行了超过1700万人的检测，当局目前正在石家庄以及邢台和廊坊等城市进行第二轮大规模检测。  河北省当局现在敦促居民待在家里，官员们被派往城市和农村地区以执行措施，并确保人民不会跨省进入北京。  为应对人们认为的威胁，北京当局在首都最外围的大兴区确认病例后，加大了检测力度，并于周三宣布将关闭附近的两个地铁站，直至另行通知。  在吉林省东北部，有102起案件与所谓的“超级传播者”有关。 一个从他的家乡黑龙江省旅行的推销员。

(CNN)China is rushing to build a massive quarantine camp that can house more than 4,000 people, after an outbreak of  Covid-19 this month that has left tens of millions of people under strict lockdown.
 The quarantine camp is located on the outskirts of Shijiazhuang, the provincial capital of Hebei province, which surrounds the country's capital, Beijing.
 China has largely contained the spread of the virus, with much of the country returning to normal. However, a sudden rise in  cases has alarmed officials and raised concerns ahead of the Lunar New Year, the county's most important annual festival, during which hundreds of millions of people are expected to travel to visit family members.
 Officials in Shijiazhuang, where the outbreak is centered, have initiated mass testing and strict lockdowns, moving entire villages into centralized quarantine facilities in a bid to curb the spread of the virus.
  The new quarantine camp will house close contacts of confirmed Covid-19 patients, as authorities continue an extensive contact tracing and testing program.
  It was originally planned to house 3,000 people, but has since been expanded to a capacity of 4,160. More than 4,000 construction workers performed "six days' and nights' work" to complete the first phase, said Shijiazhuang Deputy Mayor Meng Xianghong on Tuesday.
 Authorities began construction on January 13 and the first section of the camp is now complete and ready for use, while construction continues on the second phase, according to state-owned broadcaster CCTV.
 Each prefabricated room is expected to measure 18 square meters (around 194 square feet), and will come with an en-suite bathroom and shower, desks, chairs, beds, Wi-Fi, and a television set, according to CCTV.
  The ambitious task is reminiscent of earlier efforts during the initial stages of the pandemic, during which authorities built several medical facilities from scratch, including a 1,000-bed hospital in just  10 days.
 On Tuesday, China reported 103 new confirmed cases and 58 asymptomatic infections, which are counted separately, spread out across four provinces. Hebei province now has a total of 818 active locally transmitted cases, and more than 200 asymptomatic infections, according to the  provincial health commission.
 Last Wednesday, a patient died in Hebei -- the country's first Covid-19 related death in 242 days.
 The total number of confirmed Covid-19 cases in mainland China now stands at 88,557, while the official death toll is 4,635.
   In an effort to contain the outbreak, authorities placed Shijiazhuang under lockdown on January 8, with all 11 million residents barred from leaving the city.
 More than 20,000 citizens from 12 villages in Shijiangzhuang have since been relocated to other quarantine sites as a preventative measure, Chinese state media outlet CGTN reported last week.
 To date, more than 17 million people have been tested in Hebei, with authorities currently undertaking a second round of mass testing in Shijiazhuang and the cities of Xingtai and Langfang.
 Hebei authorities are now urging residents to stay at home, with officials sent to urban and rural areas to enforce measures and ensure people aren't traveling across the province and into Beijing.
 In response to the perceived threat, Beijing authorities have stepped up testing and screening efforts after cases were confirmed in the capital's outermost Daxing district, and announced on Wednesday they would close two nearby metro stations until further notice.
 In northeastern Jilin province, 102 cases have been  linked to a so-called "superspreader," a salesman who traveled from his home province of Heilongjiang.
title: 随着疫情持续爆发，中国将为4000人建立大规模的Covid-19隔离营
title_en: China Builds Massive Covid-19 Quarantine Camp For 4,000 People as Outbreak Continues

网络浏览器制造商Opera斥资1000万美元收购了GameMaker Studio 2游戏引擎制造商YoYo Games，并且还开设了Opera游戏部门。
 这项交易强调了Opera通过游戏社区来区别其网络浏览器Opera GX的努力。 Opera本身在全球范围内有超过3.8亿人在使用其Web浏览器，但是截至12月，新的Opera GX游戏浏览器每月有700万活跃用户，比去年同期增长350％。
  Opera浏览器执行副总裁Krystian Kolondra在接受GamesBeat采访时表示，Opera游戏将专注于扩大Opera在游戏中的功能和获利机会。公开交易的Opera总部位于挪威的奥斯陆，而YoYo Games总部位于苏格兰的邓迪。
 Kolondra说：“几年前，我们决定进行创新，而不必与浏览器的大众市场竞争。” “我们专注于实际增长且有意义的某些细分市场。所以我们决定去玩游戏。”
 GameMaker Studio 2在开发2D游戏的工具中找到了自己的定位，并且被很少接受编程等​​技能训练的人使用。 Kolondra说，这项技术已经达到了人们对制作自己的游戏和玩游戏同样感兴趣的地步。 Opera还具有内置的加密货币钱包。
 到2020年，YoYo Games拥有40万新的注册用户。这些用户为流行的恐怖游戏《弗雷迪的五夜》制作了超过12,000个mod。
  “这降低了任何人开始制作游戏的入门门槛，” Kolondra说。 
这些游戏可以在广泛的网络支持平台上运行，包括PC，移动iOS / Android设备和控制台。
 YoYo Games总经理Stuart Poole将与技术主管Russell Kay一起留在公司。 YoYo Games使用GameMaker引擎已有多年历史，自2012年以来已被下载超过1000万次。
 Opera GX，YoYo Games和GameMaker将在Opera Gaming的统领下团结在一起，专注于在游戏，游戏开发和浏览器体验方面进行创新。该团队将致力于使GameMaker更适合新手用户使用，并将针对商业游戏工作室的需求进行定制。 Opera在2019年推出了Opera GX游戏浏览器。它具有自定义选项，声音效果，背景音乐，以游戏为灵感的设计，以及CPU，内存和网络带宽限制器，使浏览器的资源消耗更少。
 GameMaker带来的一些热门游戏包括：下雨风险，Undertale和Hyper Light Drifter。这些游戏已经售出了数百万本。 Kolondra表示，该公司不打算制作仅在Opera GX浏览器上运行的独家游戏，因为其目标是围绕Opera GX用户创建社区，并使想制作跨平台游戏的人也可以使用GameMaker Pro 2。
 Kolondra表示，良好的浏览器性能甚至对于2D游戏也是必不可少的，要在移动设备和笔记本电脑上保持这种性能以及可变的带宽和连接性，就需要在传统应用程序孤岛之外进行思考。他说，构建好的游戏和好的浏览器之间的界限已经消失了多年-随着互联网连接之间的游戏交互性不断提高，浏览器越来越多功能和复杂。
 Kolondra说，Opera GX受到了那些不想仅仅通过运行网络浏览器而牺牲性能的玩家的好评。迄今为止，它的收视率已超过一百万。
 “对我们来说，游戏现在更像是一个社区，”科隆德拉说。 “这将加速我们的增长，因为这将是一个非常独特的价值主张。 GameMaker Studio使人们可以轻松创建游戏。”
note_en:
Web browser maker  Opera has acquired  YoYo Games, maker of the GameMaker Studio 2 game engine, for $10 million, and it has also launched its Opera Gaming division.
 The deal underscores  Opera‘s efforts to differentiate its web browser, Opera GX, through a gaming community. Opera itself has more than 380 million people using its web browsers worldwide, but the new Opera GX gaming browser has seven million monthly active users as of December, up 350% from a year earlier.
  Opera Gaming will focus on expanding Opera’s capabilities and monetization opportunities in the gaming, said Krystian Kolondra, executive vice president of browsers at Opera, in an interview with GamesBeat. Publicly traded Opera is based in Oslo, Norway, while  YoYo Games is based in Dundee, Scotland.
 “A couple years ago, we made the decision to produce innovation and not have to compete with mass market in browsers,” Kolondra said. “We focus on some segments that actually grow and are meaningful. So we decided to go after gaming.”
 GameMaker Studio 2 found its niche as a tool for making 2D games, and it’s being used by people who have very little training in skills such as programming. Kolondra said the tech has hit a point where people are becoming just as interested in making their own games as playing them. Opera also has a built-in cryptocurrency wallet.
 YoYo Games had more than 400,000 new registered users in 2020. Those users have created more than 12,000 mods for Five Nights at Freddy’s, the popular horror game.
  “It lowers the barrier to entry for anyone to start making their games,” Kolondra said.
 The games can run across a wide range of web-supported platforms including PCs, mobile iOS/Android devices, and consoles.
 Stuart Poole, general manager at YoYo Games, will remain with the business alongside technical lead Russell Kay. YoYo Games has worked on the GameMaker engine for years, and it has been downloaded more than 10 million times since 2012.
 Opera GX, YoYo Games, and GameMaker will unite under Opera Gaming, focusing on innovating across the gaming, game development, and browser experience. The team will concentrate on making GameMaker more accessible to novice users, and it will also tailor it for the needs of commercial game studios. Opera launched its Opera GX gaming browser in 2019. It features customization options, sound effects, background music, a gaming-inspired design, as well as CPU, memory, and network bandwidth limiters that make the browser less resource-hungry .
 Some of the big hits made with GameMaker include Risk of Rain, Undertale, and Hyper Light Drifter. Those games have sold millions of copies. Kolondra said the company does not plan to make exclusive games that run only with the Opera GX browser, as the goal is to create a community around Opera GX users but also make GameMaker Pro 2 available to those who want to make cross-platform games.
 Kolondra said that good browser performance is essential to even 2D gaming, and sustaining that performance across mobile devices and laptops as well as variable bandwidth and connectivity requires thinking outside of traditional application silos. The line between building good games and good browsers has been eroding for years — with gaming interactivity across internet connections ramping up and browsers growing more multi-function and sophisticated, he said.
 Opera GX has been getting good reviews from players who don’t want to sacrifice performance just by having a web browser running, Kolondra said. It has more than a million ratings to date.
 “For us, gaming is more like a community now,” Kolondra said. “This is going to accelerate our growth as it will be a very unique value proposition. GameMaker Studio makes it easy for people to create games.”
title: Opera以1000万美元的价格收购了YoYo Games，并成立了Opera Gaming部门以通过其游戏收益获利； Opera GX游戏浏览器在12月的MAU达到700万，同比增长350％
title_en: Opera acquires YoYo Games for $10M and launches Opera Gaming division to monetize its gaming efforts; Opera GX gaming browser had 7M MAUs in Dec., up 350% YoY

1月20日，今天中午，罗谢尔·瓦伦斯基（Rochelle Walensky）博士将接任疾病控制与预防中心的主任-她的首要任务之一就是设法消除特朗普政府对机构造成的所有伤害。
 “我如何确保在那里的人们-这些令人难以置信的科学家，这些令人难以置信的整个职业生涯的公务员-理解并感受到我们应该赋予他们的价值？他们已经减少。我认为他们被蒙住了嘴，还没有听说过科学，”瓦伦斯基周二在简短而广泛的采访中说。 “这家享誉全球的顶级代理商在过去四年中并没有得到真正的赞赏，而在过去一年中确实表现出色。因此，我必须解决该问题。”
 她这样做的计划的一部分是让那些科学家保持警惕，并将他们的科学成果公之于众，从而有所作为。她说，这混入下一个挑战：“我们显然需要使这个国家脱离COVID和当前的大流行危机。”这也将需要与公众，州和地方卫生当局以及国会议员加强交流。
 瓦伦斯基（Walensky）是哈佛医学院的医学教授，麻省总医院和百老汇妇女医院的传染病科主任。她今天接任联邦机构的10,000多名员工，将对政府卫生工作陌生。她说：“我将拥有从外面进来并能够进来说'这感觉真的很糟'的所有好处。”她补充说，对于她需要的任何机构知识，她都将依靠长期的职业职员。
 像总统当选人拜登，Walensky将立即专注于帮助理顺和加快COVID-19疫苗部署和达到了100个百万次到武器在头100天的政府的目标。根据美国疾病预防控制中心（CDC）截至1月19日的数据，政府已分发了超过3100万剂疫苗，但已分发的药物不到1600万剂。
     她说，加快步伐将涉及帮助各州设定正确的疫苗接种资格条件，这些条件不是很严格，以至于最终剂量的疫苗不用放在冰箱中就可以使用，或者宽松得使在不堪重负的疫苗接种场所外排起长队。拜登（Biden）团队还旨在促进疫苗供应的生产，增加可以提供疫苗的人数以及增加疫苗接种地点的数量。
 在谈到最近几天最紧迫的话题之一时，瓦伦斯基还说，她和拜登团队关注的是有关冠状病毒的变异体，这些变异体在世界各地（包括美国）都在流行。该小组正在努力“大刀阔斧”地加强监视工作，包括与行业和学术实验室合作，以便他们可以跟踪在美国境内或进入美国并开始传播的任何变体。 
她指出，变体最主要担心的是它们是否更容易传播，是否引起更严重的疾病和死亡，是否使疗法和疫苗的有效性降低。我们发现各种变体似乎更容易在人与人之间传播。但是我们还没有证据表明这些变体正在增加疾病和死亡人数，或者它们正在逃避疫苗和疗法。
 她说：“我认为，有关变体的好消息是疫苗的功效是如此之高和如此之高，以至于我们有一定的缓冲能力。”即使实验室研究表明，疫苗对变体的效果不如最初的菌株，“我们仍可能会获得很好的疫苗。”她的观点得到了许多其他专家的回应，他们预测，冠状病毒要发展到完全胜过免疫反应还需要数年的时间。
 她补充说：“我只想提醒人们：我们几乎没有一种疫苗能达到95％的有效率”，就像COVID-19疫苗一样。 “所以，在我们惊慌地说‘好吧，如果它不能对抗该变种，我应该真的得到疫苗吗？’—它可以对抗该变种。会是95％吗？也许。会是百分之七十吗？也许。但是我们的流感疫苗并非每年70％有效，我们仍然可以使用。因此，我对这些变体的发展方式非常乐观。”
note_en:
Midday today, January 20, Dr. Rochelle Walensky will take over as director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention—and one of her top priorities will be to try to undo all the harms done to the agency by the Trump administration.
 “How is it that I make sure that the people who are there—these incredible scientists, these incredible civil servants for their entire career—understand and feel the value that we should be giving them? They have been diminished. I think they’ve been muzzled, that science hasn’t been heard,” Walensky said in  a brief, but wide-ranging interview with JAMA Tuesday. “This top-tier agency—world renowned—hasn’t really been appreciated over the last four years and really markedly over the last year. So, I have to fix that.”
 Part of her plan to do that is unmuzzling those scientists and getting their science out to the public where it can make a difference. And that blends into the next challenge: “We obviously need to get this country out of COVID and the current pandemic crisis,” she said. And that will also entail increasing communication with the public, as well as state and local health authorities and members of Congress.
 Walensky—a professor of medicine at Harvard Medical School and chief of the Infectious Disease division at Massachusetts General Hospital and Brigham and Women's Hospital—will be new to government health work when she takes over the federal agency of over 10,000 staff today. “I will have all of the benefit of coming in from the outside and being able to look in and say ‘This feels really broken,’” she said. For any institutional knowledge she’ll need, she’ll rely on long-standing career staff, she added.
 Like President-elect Biden, Walensky will immediately focus on helping smooth out and speed up the COVID-19 vaccine rollout—and reaching the administration’s goal of getting 100 million shots into arms in the first 100 days. According to CDC data as of January 19, the government has distributed more than 31 million doses, but less than 16 million have been administered.
     Picking up the pace will involve helping states set the right eligibility conditions for vaccination—conditions that aren’t so restrictive that vaccine doses end up sitting unused in refrigerators, or so loose that there are long lines outside overwhelmed vaccination sites, she said. The Biden team also aims to boost manufacturing for vaccine supplies, increase the number of people who can provide vaccines, and increase the number of places where vaccines are administered.
 Addressing one of the most pressing topics in recent days, Walensky also said she and the Biden team have their eye on the concerning coronavirus variants that are popping up in various places around the world—including the US. The team is working to “dramatically” bolster surveillance efforts, including partnering with industry and academic labs, so that they can track any variants that develop in or enter the US and begin to spread.
 The main things to worry about with variants are if they spread more easily, if they cause more severe disease and deaths, and if they make therapies and vaccines less effective, she noted. We’ve seen variants that seem to spread from person to person more easily. But we have yet to see evidence that variants are increasing disease and death or that they’re evading vaccines and therapies.
 “I think the good news with regard to the variants is that the efficacy of the vaccines is so good and so high that we have a little bit of a cushion,” she said. Even if lab studies show vaccines aren’t quite as effective against a variant as the initial strain, “we’ll probably still end up with quite a good vaccine.” Her point has been echoed by many other experts who predict it will take years before the coronavirus evolves to completely outwit immune responses.
 “I just want to remind people: almost no vaccine we have is 95 percent effective” like the COVID-19 vaccines, she added. “So, before we panic and say ‘well, should I really get the vaccine if it’s not going to work against the variant?’—It’s going to work against the variant. Will it be 95 percent? Maybe. Will it be 70 percent? Maybe. But our flu vaccines aren’t 70 percent effective every year and we still get them. So, I’m really optimistic about how these variants are going to go.”
title: 拜登疾病预防控制中心主任的首要任务：修复特朗普所破坏的一切
title_en: First task for Biden’s CDC director: Fix everything Trump broke
